todos.title: Optimize KernelBuilder.build to pack slots into VLIW bundles
todos.micro_goal: Implement the scheduled design contract within this scope: Modify build() to group slots into instruction bundles respecting SLOT_LIMITS instead of one bundle per slot.

risk_evidence.quote: Modify build() to group slots into instruction bundles respecting SLOT_LIMITS instead of one bundle per slot.
risk_evidence.explanation: Changing how instructions are bundled affects the control flow of the simulator (how many cycles each instruction takes), which is a structural change to execution.

todos.probe_plan: Implement a guarded version of build() that packs slots into bundles but keeps the original behavior if SLOT_LIMITS are exceeded or if correctness fails. Start with a simple packing strategy.

probe_diff_contract.observation: CYCLES count from do_kernel_test and test pass rate

invariant_evidence: SLOT_LIMITS are respected by the new packing logic
Instruction order is preserved within each engine
Debug/pause instructions remain in their original positions relative to other instructions
preserved_invariants: Instruction count per engine must not exceed SLOT_LIMITS
Order of instructions must remain identical for correctness
todos.edit_scope: Modify build() to group slots into instruction bundles respecting SLOT_LIMITS instead of one bundle per slot.

validator.failure_condition: CYCLES >= 147734 OR test failure OR SLOT_LIMITS violation detected

todos.correctness_rationale: By preserving instruction order and respecting slot limits, the semantic behavior of the kernel remains identical. The only change is execution parallelism within cycles.
todos.fallback_plan: If packing fails to reduce cycles or breaks correctness, revert to original build() implementation.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Shrink to a simpler packing strategy (e.g., pack only ALU instructions) or revert entirely if the probe fails.

last_non_budget_attempt.reason: Optimize KernelBuilder.build to pack slots into VLIW bundles
